record.field: Harbor is a CNCF-graduated, open-source container registry that stores and secures OCI images and artifacts with RBAC, vulnerability scanning, signing and replication.

Harbor is a CNCF-graduated, open-source container registry that stores and secures OCI images and artifacts with RBAC, vulnerability scanning, signing and replication.

Harbor extends a plain OCI registry with the governance an organization needs to run container images in production. It groups images into projects with role-based access control and quotas, scans every artifact for known vulnerabilities, signs and verifies content, and replicates images between registries and remote Harbor instances. Graduated by the CNCF and battle-tested at scale, it is the standard self-hosted alternative to hosted registries like Docker Hub, Amazon ECR or GitHub Container Registry.

Key features
  • Private registry for Docker/OCI images, Helm charts and arbitrary artifacts, organized into projects
  • Role-based access control with projects, quotas, robot accounts and OIDC/LDAP single sign-on
  • Vulnerability scanning with Trivy plus CVE allow-lists to block risky images
  • Content trust: image signing and verification with Cosign / Notation
  • Replication policies to mirror and sync images across registries and remote Harbors
  • Tag retention, tag immutability, garbage collection and event webhooks
Best for

Platform and DevOps teams that need a governed, self-hosted registry as the trusted image source for their CI/CD pipelines and clusters. Harbor is an advanced, multi-service stack: it must run behind real HTTPS, is officially initialized with its own installer (which generates the per-service secrets, certificates and configuration), needs roughly 4 GB of RAM, and should have retention and garbage-collection policies in place since image storage grows quickly.

services: postgresql: image: goharbor/harbor-db:${HARBOR_TAG:-v2.12.2} restart: unless-stopped environment: POSTGRES_PASSWORD: {{ db_password }} volumes: - ./data/database:/var/lib/postgresql/data healthcheck: test: - CMD-SHELL - pg_isready -U postgres interval: 30s timeout: 5s retries: 5 start_period: 20s expose: - 5432 container_name: '{{ INSTANCE_CODE }}_postgresql' networks: - internal redis: image: goharbor/redis-photon:${HARBOR_TAG:-v2.12.2} restart: unless-stopped volumes: - ./data/redis:/var/lib/redis expose: - 6379 container_name: '{{ INSTANCE_CODE }}_redis' networks: - internal registry: image: goharbor/registry-photon:${HARBOR_TAG:-v2.12.2} restart: unless-stopped environment: REGISTRY_HTTP_SECRET: {{ registry_http_secret }} volumes: - ./data/registry:/storage - ./harbor-registry.yml:/etc/registry/config.yml:ro depends_on: - redis expose: - 5000 container_name: '{{ INSTANCE_CODE }}_registry' networks: - internal registryctl: image: goharbor/harbor-registryctl:${HARBOR_TAG:-v2.12.2} restart: unless-stopped environment: CORE_SECRET: {{ core_secret }} JOBSERVICE_SECRET: {{ jobservice_secret }} REGISTRY_HTTP_SECRET: {{ registry_http_secret }} volumes: - ./data/registry:/storage - ./harbor-registry.yml:/etc/registry/config.yml:ro - ./harbor-registryctl.yml:/etc/registryctl/config.yml:ro depends_on: - registry expose: - 8080 container_name: '{{ INSTANCE_CODE }}_registryctl' networks: - internal core: image: goharbor/harbor-core:${HARBOR_TAG:-v2.12.2} restart: unless-stopped environment: CORE_SECRET: {{ core_secret }} JOBSERVICE_SECRET: {{ jobservice_secret }} CORE_URL: http://core:8080 JOBSERVICE_URL: http://jobservice:8080 REGISTRY_URL: http://registry:5000 REGISTRYCTL_URL: http://registryctl:8080 REGISTRY_CONTROLLER_URL: http://registryctl:8080 TOKEN_SERVICE_URL: http://core:8080/service/token PORTAL_URL: http://portal:8080 EXT_ENDPOINT: https://{{ instance.full_domain }} LOG_LEVEL: info CONFIG_PATH: /etc/core/app.conf DATABASE_TYPE: postgresql POSTGRESQL_HOST: postgresql POSTGRESQL_PORT: "5432" POSTGRESQL_USERNAME: postgres POSTGRESQL_PASSWORD: {{ db_password }} POSTGRESQL_DATABASE: registry POSTGRESQL_SSLMODE: disable POSTGRESQL_MAX_IDLE_CONNS: "100" POSTGRESQL_MAX_OPEN_CONNS: "900" HARBOR_ADMIN_PASSWORD: {{ harbor_admin_password }} CSRF_KEY: {{ csrf_key }} REGISTRY_STORAGE_PROVIDER_NAME: filesystem REGISTRY_CREDENTIAL_USERNAME: harbor_registry_user REGISTRY_CREDENTIAL_PASSWORD: {{ registry_http_secret }} _REDIS_URL_CORE: redis://redis:6379/0 _REDIS_URL_REG: redis://redis:6379/1 CHART_CACHE_DRIVER: redis WITH_TRIVY: "false" WITH_NOTARY: "false" PERMITTED_REGISTRY_TYPES_FOR_PROXY_CACHE: docker-hub,harbor,azure-acr,aws-ecr,google-gcr,quay,docker-registry,github-ghcr,jfrog-artifactory volumes: - ./harbor-core.conf:/etc/core/app.conf:ro - ./data/core:/data depends_on: - postgresql - redis - registry expose: - 8080 container_name: '{{ INSTANCE_CODE }}_core' networks: - internal portal: image: goharbor/harbor-portal:${HARBOR_TAG:-v2.12.2} restart: unless-stopped expose: - 8080 container_name: '{{ INSTANCE_CODE }}_portal' networks: - internal jobservice: image: goharbor/harbor-jobservice:${HARBOR_TAG:-v2.12.2} restart: unless-stopped environment: CORE_SECRET: {{ core_secret }} JOBSERVICE_SECRET: {{ jobservice_secret }} CORE_URL: http://core:8080 REGISTRY_CONTROLLER_URL: http://registryctl:8080 HARBOR_ADMIN_PASSWORD: {{ harbor_admin_password }} JOBSERVICE_WEBHOOK_JOB_MAX_RETRY: "3" volumes: - ./harbor-jobservice.yml:/etc/jobservice/config.yml:ro - ./data/job_logs:/var/log/jobs depends_on: - core expose: - 8080 container_name: '{{ INSTANCE_CODE }}_jobservice' networks: - internal proxy: image: goharbor/nginx-photon:${HARBOR_TAG:-v2.12.2} restart: unless-stopped volumes: - ./harbor-nginx.conf:/etc/nginx/nginx.conf:ro depends_on: - core - portal - registry ports: - '{{ PORT_HTTP }}:8080' container_name: '{{ INSTANCE_CODE }}' networks: - internal - agz-proxy-network networks: internal: driver: bridge agz-proxy-network: external: true
Initial password for the built-in 'admin' account of the Harbor UI.
Password for the internal PostgreSQL (harbor-db) superuser.
Shared secret authenticating the core service against the other Harbor components.
Shared secret authenticating the jobservice against the core service.
Shared secret used by the registry HTTP layer and the core registry credential.
CSRF protection key for the core service (Harbor requires exactly 32 characters).

version: 0.1 log: level: info fields: service: registry storage: filesystem: rootdirectory: /storage cache: layerinfo: redis maintenance: uploadpurging: enabled: false delete: enabled: true redis: addr: redis:6379 db: 1 readtimeout: 10s writetimeout: 10s dialtimeout: 10s pool: maxidle: 100 maxactive: 500 idletimeout: 60s http: addr: :5000 debug: addr: localhost:5001 headers: X-Content-Type-Options: - nosniff validation: disabled: true compatibility: schema1: enabled: true

--- protocol: "http" port: 8080 worker_pool: workers: 10 backend: "redis" redis_pool: redis_url: "redis://redis:6379/2" namespace: "harbor_job_service_namespace" idle_timeout_second: 3600 job_loggers: - name: "STD_OUTPUT" level: "INFO" - name: "FILE" level: "INFO" settings: base_dir: "/var/log/jobs" sweeper: duration: 14 settings: work_dir: "/var/log/jobs" metric: enabled: false path: /metrics port: 8001 loggers: - name: "STD_OUTPUT" level: "INFO"

appname = Harbor runmode = prod enablegzip = true
[prod] httpport = 8080
